Tai Motion · Chair Exercises

10 Chair Exercises

A gentle, chair-supported visual guide — for strength, balance & mobility
Tai Motion

Before you begin

  • Check with your physician before starting a new exercise routine, especially if you have any concerns.
  • Use a stable, armless chair on a non-slip floor, with clear space around you.
  • Wear supportive footwear (or go barefoot on a non-slip floor) — avoid loose slippers.
  • Keep water within easy reach, and rest whenever you need to.
  • Move slowly and never force a movement — a little effort, never pain.
  • If you feel dizzy, short of breath, or any pain, stop and rest.
01

Chair-Assisted Squat

Standing · chair-supported
  1. Stand tall behind the chair, feet about shoulder-width apart, both hands resting on the backrest.
  2. Send your hips back and down as if reaching for a seat — hips bend first, then knees.
  3. Lower to a comfortable depth, keeping your knees behind your toes and your chest lifted.
  4. Press through your heels to stand tall again.
Move slowly, 8–12 times.
1
02

Chair-Assisted Reverse Lunge

Standing · chair-supported
  1. Stand tall behind the chair, both hands on the backrest for support.
  2. Step one foot back and lower into a gentle lunge, back knee reaching toward the floor.
  3. Keep your front knee stacked over your ankle.
  4. Press through the front heel to return, then repeat on the other leg.
Move slowly, 8–12 times each side.
2
03

Chair-Assisted Hip Abduction

Standing · chair-supported
  1. Stand tall behind the chair, hands on the backrest, weight on one leg.
  2. Keeping the moving leg straight and your toes pointing forward, lift it out to the side.
  3. Pause briefly at the top, then lower with control.
  4. Repeat, then switch to the other leg.
Move slowly, 8–12 times each side.
3
04

Seated Crunch

Seated
  1. Sit tall toward the front of the chair, feet flat, hands resting lightly by your ears.
  2. Draw your ribs toward your hips, curling your shoulders forward from your middle.
  3. Keep your hips and shoulders steady, and don't pull on your neck.
  4. Return to sitting tall, and repeat.
Move slowly, 8–12 times.
4
05

Seated Bicycle Crunch

Seated
  1. Sit tall, feet flat, hands resting lightly by your ears.
  2. Lift one knee and gently rotate the opposite elbow toward it.
  3. Sit tall again, then switch sides in a slow, steady rhythm.
  4. Move from your middle, never your neck.
Move slowly, 8–12 times each side.
5
06

Toe-Tap with Bicep Curl

Seated
  1. Sit tall, arms hanging at your sides, palms facing forward.
  2. Lift one foot and tap your toe to the floor in front of you.
  3. At the same time, bend both elbows to curl your hands toward your shoulders.
  4. Lower and switch feet, keeping a smooth rhythm.
Move slowly, 8–12 times each side.
6
07

Heel-Tap with Overhead Press

Seated
  1. Sit tall with your elbows bent at shoulder height, like a goalpost.
  2. Lift one foot and tap your heel to the floor in front of you.
  3. As you tap, press both hands straight up overhead.
  4. Lower and switch feet, keeping tall posture throughout.
Move slowly, 8–12 times each side.
7
08

Seated Arm Circles

Seated · optional marching
  1. Sit tall with your arms out to the sides at shoulder height, like wings.
  2. Draw slow circles with your arms — first one direction, then the other.
  3. If it feels good, gently march your feet in place as you circle.
  4. Keep your shoulders relaxed and away from your ears.
Move slowly, 8–12 circles each way.
8
09

Seated Cat–Cow

Seated
  1. Sit tall with your hands resting on your knees.
  2. Inhale, lift your chest and gently arch, squeezing your shoulder blades together (Cow).
  3. Exhale, round your back and draw your chin toward your chest (Cat).
  4. Flow slowly between the two, moving with your breath.
Move slowly, 8–12 times.
9
10

Y-T-A Raises

Seated
  1. Sit tall with your arms out to the sides, palms facing up.
  2. Reach both arms overhead into a “Y,” slightly in front of your body.
  3. Open them out to a “T,” level with your shoulders.
  4. Draw them back into an “A,” squeezing your shoulder blades — then reverse and repeat.
Move slowly, 8–12 times.
10

TaiMotion showcase

One gentle app, everything built around you:

  • Personalized meal plan — a weekly plan with a smart groceries list you can swap, tick off and shop from, while you keep an easy eye on your calories on your weight-loss journey.
  • Walking & Chair Tai Chi — over 100 follow-along videos, seated or standing, always at your own pace.
  • Simple trackers — water, weight, mood, stress, fasting and balance, so you can watch how you feel improve.
  • Academy lessons — a short, calming lesson each day to keep you learning.
  • Guided challenges — friendly little challenges to keep you moving and motivated.
